title: Xavier University of Louisiana - Full Episode | The College Tour
body:
Even from before the 2024 election, DEI was not 'popular' anymore in many corporations that experienced the downside of preferring candidates that ticked the right woke boxes over competent and qualified ones. And common sense management that values performance over faux political correctness, has been slashing DEI departments and scrapping their woke policies, as the demoralization effect of the DEI stupidity chased real talent out of the company.
The writing is on the wall > DEI is dying, and those companies that still endorse it will go down with it.

title: #XULA | Why I left Xavier University of Louisiana ?|
body: I dropped out of XULA yrs ago.  It was more my inabilities and not XULAs deficiency.  I worked for six years, eventually went to a state Univ and on to Medical School. The side journey was interesting and necessary for my growth
author: @joeass883

title: #XULA | Why I left Xavier University of Louisiana ?|
body: I got into 9 schools nationwide and XULA was the most affordable I got into. I attended site UNseen and enjoyed my time there. I was supported by my advisors. And there were multiple opportunities to get additional scholarships via the English office poetry competitions, I visited every office I could and asked about scholarships and got an additional  $12,000 per year. This young lady is gorgeous! Just had to sidetrack about that. But I digress to return and say I always found it a direct correlation between the students who couldnt handle the work and the complainers. None of us who made the Deans List and utilized all the services it has to offer had complaints. This school is ranked #1 for the most African Americans who go onto complete medical school for a reason. I have no doubts this young lady will have a bright future. But she had her heart set on Hampton and shouldve went there. FAMU is a GREAT school and many of our XULA peeps have FAMU spouses! Im proud of this scholar regardless. But XULA is bomb. And closed mouths dont get fed. Students go to every office even if not your dept and demand help!
author: @289dancer

title: #XULA | Why I left Xavier University of Louisiana ?|
body: I remember watching this my freshman year at XULA now I'm back as a sophomore and 100% agree with everything you said. I am transferring bc they full of it. They are unsympathetic and greedy for money.
author: @NabriaC

title: #XULA | Why I left Xavier University of Louisiana ?|
body: Agree, XULA is what you are able manage and prepared for.  It is a good school for many for a variety of reasons.  It is not for everyone.  Thus, choose wisely based on the top 6-10 criteria important and necessary for your growth, development and well being
author: @joeass883

title: #XULA | Why I left Xavier University of Louisiana ?|
body: Oh shit. I got a full ride to XULA and I’m worried about going. This scared the shit out of me bc it’s my best option financially. I got the Board if Trustees which covers my tuition room, board and other fees. I planned to tour it this spring break but Coronavirus happened. I want to major in biomedical engineering and bioinformatics bc they do amazing research there. My plan is to try out the school and transfer on my first year. Xavier is a good school and your experience shows me what to expect. I know I will do well no matter where I go and I can’t choose a school that will cost me too much. At worse I can transfer or just do a program that works with a partner institution. Wish me luck y’all I hope I do well.
author: @ebenezernyenwe1677

title: The Tea on XULA PreMed Life: Answers to Your Most Asked Questions
body: Exactly what you stated in the first 4 minutes is not only amazing but its exactly what students NEED to DO to be successful! This is why students are SO prepared for medical school by the time they go through XULA because we weren't spoonfed when we were there. And that's not a problem nor is it limited to HBCUs. I went to a PWI for graduate school and the undergraduate blacks that I mentored were LOST, were NOT supported, and were LEFT to fend for themselves. This is not an HBCU vs PWI issue. ALL students who are NOT prepared to SELF-ADVOCATE will have an issue with trying to figure out how to sink or swim. I was in every office I didn't care what the major was, asking questions every semester and was a 4.0 student, yes in medical subjects and yes the medical profession is easy to me decades later THANKS TO THIS FINE INSTITUTION. I think students are not prepared to make the jump from easy high school read whatever whenever to omg I actually have to hunker down and study and know this material without someone holding my hand. That's the BEAUTY of college, to wake us up so we GROW UP. Great video. And you were so noble to wait until you felt led to release your perspective on this AMAZING alma mater of ours.
author: @Ginger_Spicy_Candor
